meaning: adj.墨丘利神的,水星的,雄辩机智的,活泼善变的,水银的n.水银剂,汞剂
examples: Emily's mercurial temperament made her difficult to live with.
In no sense mercurial, these great fortunes survived the financial panics that destroyed lesser ones.
The children's mercurial natures were in sharp contrast to the even-tempered dispositions of their parents.
Banerjee herself is a mercurial leader and not known to be a great administrator.
Douglas, a mercurial man who once aspired to the presidency, became a free-speech champion.
